barRaise.topFinding: Tenth consecutive daily review with zero application-code findings, and the third straight day with no commits (HEAD is still 3d9b480 / v0.18.4, last moved 2026-06-14). The 06-17 canonical audit reads aligned with one LOW, and that LOW is already half-fixed: PROJECT_SPEC.md is modified in the working tree correcting the backlog line the audit flagged, but the edit is uncommitted and the dashboard still says workTreeClean true. The three MEDIUMs are identical to the last five reviews and none is a bug: the 864 MB .git, the work queue split across three project-ID spellings, and a 98%-complete tool still on a daily cadence. The highest-leverage move is unchanged and now overdue: cut v1.0, settle the strip-or-keep .git call, and drop Apothecary to weekly review so the cadence stops out-running the work.

actions.title: Decide and close the 864 MB `.git` bloat: `git filter-repo --strip-blobs-bigger-than 5M` + force-push, or a "Won't fix" locked decision in PROJECT_SPEC section 8.
Cut v1.0, mark "Symbol + label polish" done, and drop the project to weekly review. Fifth review carrying this.

Normalize the queue to one project ID (`yaa`) and a prompt-bearing field; backfill the `YaApothecary` item and close the shipped `yaa-commit-release-automsg-2026-06-12`. (Orchestration side; read-only here.)
